loop.field2: A delicate brioche toast topped with fresh sea urchin, black truffle shavings, and a whisper of truffle oil.
Slow-cooked duck leg in junmai sake, served with pickled daikon and yuzu kosho glaze.
Charred Japanese eggplant glazed with red miso, topped with toasted sesame and scallions.
Thinly sliced salmon cured in kombu, served with grated wasabi and yuzu zest.

elements.content: Born in Kyoto and trained under masters in Tokyo, Chef Hiroshi began his career in traditional kaiseki before venturing across the globe—from Paris to San Francisco—to absorb new techniques and perspectives. His philosophy centers on *seasonality, balance, and reverence for ingredients*—honor the ingredient, serve the season, and let tradition guide innovation. At our table, every dish is a quiet conversation between heritage and modernity.

loop.field1: Sourcing fresh seafood at Toyosu Market

His philosophy centers on *seasonality, balance, and reverence for ingredients*—honor the ingredient, serve the season, and let tradition guide innovation. At our table, every dish is a quiet conversation between heritage and modernity.
